title: crepis runcinata subsp. glauca
intro: Crepis runcinata subsp. glauca is a plant species, commonly known as a type of hawk's beard, within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crepis runcinata subsp. glauca include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal compounds.
